How it works
  • Chaldean numerology assigns each letter a value from 1-8 (no 9)
  • Name Number is derived from your full name
  • Destiny Number is derived from your date of birth
  • Master numbers 11 and 22 are shown without further reduction

Nakshatra Speaks
Provides astrological predictions based on Vedic astrology for guidance purposes only.
Results may vary based on many factors. This is not a substitute for medical advice.
Always consult your doctor for medical decisions regarding delivery timing.
Muhurta windows are calculated using Nirayana (Lahiri) Ayanamsha.
Please use your discretion when making birth planning decisions.

Shastra Explanation
🕉️
Child Longevity (Ayurdaya)
Vedic Astrological Explanation
The longevity of a child born cannot be determined with certainty until they have completed their first twelve years of life.
📖 Causes of Shortened Longevity
Effect of Past-Life Karmic Sins
The child's longevity may be diminished due to sinful deeds performed in a previous life — either by the child themselves or by the parents in their past lives.
Balagraha Afflictions
Balagraha doshas active during the first twelve years of life can also pose a risk to the child's health and longevity.
📜 Balarista Proof — Shloka Explanation
Shloka (Sanskrit)
Ādye catuṣke jananī kṛtādyai rmadhye ca pitrārjita pāpasaṅghaiḥ ।
Bāla stadanyāsu catuḥ śaratsu svakīyadoṣaiḥ samupaiti nāśam ॥
0–4
Yrs
Mother's Karmic Effect
Due to the fruits of sinful deeds committed by the mother in past lives, Balarista may occur to the child within the first four years of life.
4–8
Yrs
Father's Karmic Effect
Due to the accumulated sinful deeds of the father, Balarista may affect the child between the ages of four and eight years.
8–12
Yrs
Child's Own Karmic Effect
Due to the child's own sinful deeds from a past life, Balarista may strike between the ages of eight and twelve years.
⚠️ Inauspicious Birth Conditions
Regardless of an otherwise auspicious birth time, if birth occurs during any of the following special times or circumstances, the horoscope will yield inauspicious results:
1. Malefic Tithis, Karanas & Yogas
• Birth during Bhadra Karana (Vishti) and Amavasya tithi.
• Birth on Krishna Paksha Chaturdashi (monthly Shivaratri).
• Birth during Surya Sankramana (solar ingress).
• Birth during highly malefic yogas like Vyatipatam, or during Yamaghanta period.
2. Nakshatra & Gandanta Doshas
• Child born in the same nakshatra as the father or elder sibling.
• Birth occurring at the Gandanta junction of Tithi, Nakshatra, or Lagna.
3. Birth Order Dosha — Trikuta Dosha
• A daughter born after three consecutive sons.
• A son born after three consecutive daughters.
4. Abnormal Deliveries (Vikruta Prasava)
• Breech presentation — feet-first delivery.
• Birth with the umbilical cord wrapped around the neck.
📌 According to Maharshi Parashara, even if a horoscope contains many auspicious yogas, the presence of the above tithi, nakshatra, yoga, or delivery anomalies will nullify them and render the birth inauspicious. Hence, prescribed Dosha Nivarana Shantis must be performed without fail.
🌑 1. Amavasya Birth Dosha — Shanti Procedure
A child born on Amavasya tithi may bring poverty and hardship to the parents. A Shanti puja must be performed according to scriptural prescription.
1. Kalasha Installation & Puja Procedure
Fill a Kalasha with water and place bark, roots, and shoots of Juvi, Ravi, Marri, Mango, and Neem trees inside. Add Pancharatnas and wrap with a red cloth. Consecrate the Kalasha water with 'Apohishtha' and 'Sarvesamudra' mantras. Place copper or silver idols of Sun and Moon and perform Shodashopachara puja.
2. Homa Procedure
Perform homa 108 times (or at least 28 times) with Savitru mantras and 'Somadhenu' mantras.
3. Sankalpa & Dana (Charity)
Parents should sit with the child during puja. After completion, feed Brahmins and offer silver, gold, and cows as Dakshina.
✅ Through these Shanti homa rituals, all doshas for a child born on Amavasya are removed and complete wellbeing is assured.
🌙 2. Krishna Chaturdashi Birth Dosha — Shanti Procedure
If the entire duration of Krishna Paksha Chaturdashi tithi is divided into 6 parts, each part gives a different result for the birth.
Part Result
First Part✅ Auspicious
Second PartDanger to Father
Third PartDanger to Mother
Fourth PartDanger to Maternal Uncle
Fifth PartDestruction of Lineage
Sixth Part⚠️ Wealth loss / Danger to Child
Shiva Idol Worship
A gold idol of Lord Shiva must be commissioned — seated on Nandi (bull), with a crescent moon mark, three eyes, and Varada-Abhaya mudras.
Puja, Abhisheka & Homa
Perform Rudrabhisheka with 'Tryambakam' mantra and Navagraha puja. Perform tilahoma 108 times. After homa, sprinkle Kalasha water on parents and child (Marjana).
✅ By performing Krishna Chaturdashi Shanti, all doshas are removed and the family and child receive complete wellbeing.
⭐ 3. Eka Nakshatra Birth Dosha — Shanti Procedure
When a child is born in the same nakshatra as the parents or siblings, it is called "Eka Nakshatra Janana." According to Maharshi Parashara, this dosha may result in one or two family members facing mortal danger.
1. Auspicious Timing
This Shanti should be performed on a good day free from Rikta tithi and Amavasya — on a day with auspicious tithi, good nakshatra, Tarabala, and Chandrabala.
2. Idol Installation & Homa Procedure
Place the birth nakshatra idol in the northeast direction of the fire altar. Perform homa 108 times with ghee and sesame mixed with nakshatra mantras.
3. Sprinkling & Charity
The priest should sprinkle Kalasha water on the parents, siblings, and child. Feed Brahmins and offer Dakshina as per one's ability.
✅ Through Eka Nakshatra Dosha Shanti, the child, siblings, and parents will live long lives in happiness and good health.
🔱 4. Three-Fold Gandanta Doshas — Timing & Remedies
The junction periods of Tithi, Nakshatra, and Lagna are called "Gandanta times." Birth during these periods can cause serious afflictions.
Shloka (Sanskrit)
pūrṇanandāhyayostidyo sandhirnāḍī dvayaṃ sadā gaṇḍāntaṃ mṛtyudaṃ janma yātrodvāha vratādiṣu
— Narada Maharshi
Gandanta Type Duration Remedy (Dana)
Tithi Gandanta4 Ghadiyas🐂 Vrishabha Danam
Nakshatra Gandanta2 Ghadiyas🐄 Go Danam
Lagna Gandanta1 Ghadiya🪙 Suvarna Danam
🚨 Abhukta Moola Ganda (Most Severe)
The last 5 ghadiyas of Jyeshtha nakshatra + the first 8 ghadiyas of Moola nakshatra is an extremely dangerous junction. Dosha remediation is absolutely mandatory.
Key Rules
• No Darshana: The father must not see the newborn until the puja is complete.
• If born in first part — the father must also take a holy bath immediately.
• If born in second part — the child and mother must be bathed at the same time.
Shloka (Sanskrit)
tathaiva tithi gaṇḍāntaṃ nāstīndau balaśālinī tathaiva lagnagaṇḍāntaṃ nāsti jīve balānvite
— Shakalya Samhita
✅ Through Shanti homa rituals, all Gandanta doshas are completely removed — longevity and prosperity are enhanced.
🌘 5. Eclipse Birth Dosha — Remedies
Children born during a solar or lunar eclipse may face financial difficulties, mental anguish, and health problems in life.
Eclipse Idol Colour
☀️ Solar EclipseGolden Sun idolRed flowers & cloth
🌙 Lunar EclipseSilver Moon idolWhite flowers & cloth
🐉 RahuLead Rahu idolBlack flowers & cloth
Mantras & Homa Materials
• For Sun: 'Akrishnena' mantra — Jilleda samidhas
• For Moon: 'Imandeva' mantra — Moduga samidhas
• For Rahu: 'Kayanashcitra' mantra — Durva grass samidhas
✅ By performing Grahana Shanti, all doshas for the child born during the eclipse are removed and health & longevity are restored.
🔥 6. Bhadra & Malefic Yoga Birth Dosha — Remedies
Birth during Bhadra Karana, Kshaya Tithi, Vyatipata Yoga, or Yamaghanta period causes severe doshas.
Recurrence Rule
Whenever the same malefic yoga recurs in the horoscope, this Shanti must be performed again.
Auspicious Muhurta & Worship
• Perform Navagraha Yajna along with Kula Daiva worship.
• Perform Rudrabhishekam at a Shiva temple.
• Pradakshinas around the Peepal tree (Ashwattha) increase longevity.
• Perform Sri Mahavishnu Hari mantra Homa 108 times.
✅ Through these Shanti procedures, all afflictions are removed and the child gains increased longevity and complete wellbeing.
👶 7. Trikuta Dosha — Birth Order Shanti
Whether a daughter is born after three consecutive sons, or a son after three consecutive daughters, it brings misfortune to both family lineages.
Worship & Mantra Recitation
Invoke and worship the Navagrahas and Brahma-Vishnu-Maheshwara. Chant Shanti Sukthas and Rudra Sukthas.
Homa Procedure
Perform homa 1008 times (or at minimum 108 times) offering samidhas, sesame, and cow's ghee. Conclude with Purnahuti.
Acharya Honour
The father must offer gold (Suvarna) and a milch cow (Go Danam) to the chief priest as Dakshina.
✅ Through scriptural Trikuta Shanti, all ancestral calamities from birth order dosha are eliminated.
☀️ 8. Surya Sankranti Birth Dosha — Shanti Procedure
A child born during Sankranti — when the Sun transitions from one rashi to another — may face financial hardships and mental suffering.
Puja Altar Setup
In an eastward-facing sacred area, place mounds of grain, rice, and sesame on separate cloths and draw an Ashtadala Padma (eight-petal lotus).
Kalasha Installation & Puja
Set up three Kalashas with Saptamrittika, Shataushadi, and Panchagavya. Place Sun, Moon, and Sankranti Purusha idols. Worship with Tryambakam mantra and perform Mrutyunjaya japa 1008 or 108 times.
Homa Procedure
Build a homa kunda west of the Kalashas and perform homa 1008, 108, or 28 times with Tryambakam mantra. Then perform tilahoma and sprinkle Kalasha water on the child and parents.
✅ By performing Sankranti Dosha Shanti, all afflictions are removed and the child attains long life.
🙏 9. Balarista Dosha Pariharams — Traditional Remedies
These are the general remedies commonly followed by everyone, regardless of the specific dosha present.
1. Ajyavekshanam
After the child's birth, the rite in which the father, as prescribed by the shastras, first views the reflection of the newborn's face in a vessel filled with oil or ghee is called Ajyavekshanam. This rite is prescribed in the shastras for the pacification of birth doshas, the child's wellbeing, and for longevity and health.
2. Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra Japam
Chanted 108 or 1008 times. Can be performed by the parents themselves, or by learned priests, for the child's wellbeing.
3. Ayushya Homa
The principal Shanti performed for the child's longevity and health. It is usually conducted after birth, or at the time of the first birthday.
4. Navagraha Shanti
If the influence of malefic planets is strong, Navagraha puja, japam, grain-danam as indicated by the afflicted planet, or Navagraha Homam should be performed.
5. Mrityunjaya Homa
Performed as prescribed by the shastras when severe Balarista yogas are present.
6. Nakshatra Japam
Shanti related to the child's birth nakshatra.
7. Danams (Charitable Donations)
Go danam (cow), Suvarna danam (gold), Vastra danam (clothes), and Anna danam (food).
8. Rudrabhishekam
Ekadasha Rudrabhishekam, or Rudrabhishekam, performed at a Shiva temple.
9. Janmadina Ayushya Puja
Performing Ayushya Homam every year on the day of the birth nakshatra.
⏳ 10. By When Should Balarista Dosha Pariharam Be Performed?
1. It should be performed by the 11th day from the child's birth.
2. It can be performed along with Nakshatra Shanti on the day the birth star recurs after the birth.
3. It should be performed within the first 3 months.
4. It is beneficial if performed within one year, combined with remedies such as Ayushya Homam and Navagraha Shanti.
By performing these Dosha Nivarana Shantis prescribed by the Maharshis, the child attains complete health, longevity, and prosperity.
👶
Matters Related to Child Birth
Traditional guidance for post-birth ceremonies
🏠 Where the Delivery Should Take Place
The first two deliveries should take place at the mother's parental home (Puttillu), and the third delivery at the husband's home (Attagari Illu).
📛 Jatakarma & Naming Ceremony (Namakaranam)
Monday, Wednesday, Thursday & Friday; Tithis 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 11, 13; and Ashwini, Rohini, Punarvasu, Pushyami, Magha, Swati, Chitta, Anuradha, Shatabhisha, Uttara, Uttarashadha, Uttarabhadra nakshatras are auspicious.
The naming ceremony should be performed on the 11th, 21st or 29th day after birth, or within the 3rd month.
❓ Must the Name Be Chosen According to the Birth Nakshatra?
No, this is not mandatory. Names of gods and goddesses can also be chosen. Good names can be found among the Ashtottara Shatanamavali (108 names).
✂️ Keshakhandanam (First Haircut)
Tithis 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 13; Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday; and Ashwini, Mrigashira, Pushyami, Shravana, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha nakshatras are good.
An odd month is favorable for a girl child, and an even month is favorable for a boy child.
💎 Nose & Ear Piercing
To be performed in the 6th, 7th or 8th month, in Shukla Paksha, with Ashtama Shuddhi and Guru Bala present, in Vrishabha, Mithuna, Karkataka, Kanya, Dhanu or Meena lagnas, during daytime. Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, and Tithis 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 13 are excellent.
This ceremony can be performed on any nakshatra except the following 11: Bharani, Krittika, Ardra, Ashlesha, Magha, Purva Phalguni (Pubba), Purva Ashadha, Purva Bhadra, Vishakha, Jyeshtha and Moola.
Even months are favorable.
🛁 Balintha Snanam (Mother's Purification Bath)
This should ideally be performed on the 11th day after delivery. If that is not possible, Sunday, Tuesday and Thursday, along with Ashwini, Rohini, Mrigashira, the Uttara trio (Uttara Phalguni, Uttarashadha, Uttarabhadra), Hasta, Swati, Anuradha and Revati nakshatras are good.
🚪 Bringing the Newborn Home (Grihantara Pravesham)
If the delivery took place in a hospital, maternity home, or some other house, the auspicious entry performed when the mother and baby are brought to their own home for the first time is called "Puriti Sisuvu Grihantara Pravesham" (the newborn's house-entry ceremony).
Tithis 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15 are good.
Ashwini, Mrigashira, Punarvasu, Pushyami, Hasta, Chitta, Anuradha and Revati nakshatras are excellent.
Kanya, Kumbha, Tula, Mesha and Vrishchika lagnas, when under the aspect of benefic planets, are auspicious.
🛏️ Cradle Ceremony (Uyyala)
On the 21st day after birth, or in the 1st, 3rd or 5th month — avoiding Varjyam and Durmuhurtam, with Ashtama Shuddhi present, in Vrishabha, Mithuna, Karkataka, Kanya, Dhanu or Meena lagnas — the baby should be placed in the cradle with the head facing east. Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday; Tithis 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 13; and Ashwini, Rohini, Mrigashira, Punarvasu, Pushyami, Uttara, Hasta, Chitta, Anuradha, Uttarashadha, Uttarabhadra, Revati nakshatras are auspicious.
Odd months are favorable.

How to Use
🗓️
Check Birth Details
Find your Nakshatra, Rashi, Panchang & Dasha using the Nirayana (Lahiri) system
1
Tap "Check Birth Details"
On the Home screen, tap the purple card to open the Birth Details form.
2
Enter Date & Time of Birth
Select the exact day, month, year and birth time (AM/PM) from the dropdowns. Use the birth certificate for accuracy.
3
Add Place of Birth
Type the city name and select from suggestions — or tap 📍 Use My Location to auto-fill via GPS.
4
Tap "Calculate ✦"
View your Nakshatra, Pada, Rashi, Tithi, Yoga, Karana and Dasha periods — all calculated using Lahiri Ayanamsha.
5
Explore Doshas & Planetary Positions
From results, tap Check Doshas for Nakshatra dosha analysis, then explore planetary positions for deeper insights.
💡
All calculations use Nirayana (Lahiri) Ayanamsha — the standard Vedic system used across India.
⏰
Plan Auspicious Birth Time
Find the best Muhurta windows for a planned delivery using Vedic astrology
1
Tap "Plan Auspicious Birth Time"
On the Home screen, tap the gold card marked NEW to begin.
2
Read & Accept the Disclaimer
Read the important note about astrological guidance vs medical advice. Check the box and tap Continue.
3
Select Family Nakshatras
Choose Father's and Mother's Nakshatras (required). Optionally add Sibling Nakshatra for better compatibility.
4
Enter Location & Date Range
Enter the hospital city and your expected delivery date range. The app calculates auspicious windows within that period.
5
Browse the Muhurta Calendar
View ranked auspicious dates. Tap any date to see exact time windows, Nakshatra details, and compatibility score.
6
View & Share the Result
Generate and share the Muhurta report with your doctor or family for planning discussions.
⚕️
Always consult your doctor before making any delivery timing decisions. Muhurta is for astrological guidance only.
📜
Janma Patrika
Generate a full Vedic birth chart with personalized insights
1
Tap "Janma Patrika"
On the Home screen, tap the lavender card to open the Janma Patrika section.
2
Enter Birth Details
Fill in date, time, and place of birth accurately — the same details as the Birth Details section. Accuracy gives a correct chart.
3
Generate Your Patrika
The app generates your full birth chart including Lagna, Rashi, all planetary positions, Nakshatra, and personalized Vedic insights.
4
Save or Share the Patrika
Download as PDF or share with family. All saved reports appear in My Reports in the bottom navigation bar.
📁
All generated Patrikas are saved in My Reports — tap the Reports icon in the bottom nav bar anytime to view them.

Legal & Support
Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy
Nakshatra Speaks respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal information.
📥 Information We Collect
  • Name
  • Birth details entered by users
  • Email address or login information
  • Device and app usage information
  • Subscription and purchase details
🎯 How We Use Information
  • Generate astrology reports and predictions
  • Save user reports and preferences
  • Improve app performance and user experience
  • Manage subscriptions and purchases
  • Provide customer support
🔒 Data Protection
We take reasonable measures to protect user information and prevent unauthorized access, misuse, or disclosure.
🔗 Third-Party Services
The app may use trusted third-party services for:
  • Payments
  • Analytics
  • Authentication
  • Notifications
These services may collect information according to their own privacy policies.
🤝 Data Sharing
We do not sell or share your personal information with third parties except when required for app functionality, legal compliance, or payment processing.
👤 User Responsibility
Users are responsible for providing accurate birth details and maintaining the security of their accounts.
🧒 Children's Privacy
The app is intended for general use and does not knowingly collect sensitive information from children without guardian involvement.
🔄 Policy Updates
This Privacy Policy may be updated from time to time. Continued use of the app means you accept the updated policy.
📬 Contact
For questions or support, please contact us through the app support section.
🔏 Your privacy matters to us. We are committed to keeping your data safe and transparent.
Terms & Conditions
Terms & Conditions
By using Nakshatra Speaks, you agree to the following terms and conditions.
📋 Use of Service
Nakshatra Speaks provides astrology-based guidance, predictions, dosha analysis, remedies, and Muhurta suggestions for informational and spiritual purposes only.
⚖️ No Professional Advice
The app content should not be considered medical, legal, financial, psychological, or professional advice. Users must consult qualified professionals for important decisions.
👤 User Responsibility
Users are responsible for:
  • Providing accurate birth details
  • Maintaining account security
  • Using app content responsibly
💳 Payments & Subscriptions
Premium reports, subscriptions, and one-time purchases are non-transferable. Prices may change in the future. Refund policies are subject to platform rules and applicable laws.
🔒 Intellectual Property
All app content, designs, calculations, branding, and reports belong to Nakshatra Speaks and may not be copied, reproduced, or redistributed without permission.
⚠️ Limitation of Liability
Nakshatra Speaks and its creators are not responsible for:
  • Prediction outcomes
  • Personal decisions
  • Financial loss
  • Emotional distress
  • Medical issues
  • Any direct or indirect damages resulting from app usage
🔄 Service Changes
We may modify, update, suspend, or discontinue features at any time without prior notice.
✅ By using the app, you acknowledge that you have read and agreed to these Terms & Conditions.
Disclaimer
Disclaimer
Please read this disclaimer carefully before using Nakshatra Speaks.
🔮 Informational Purpose Only
Nakshatra Speaks provides astrology-based spiritual guidance and traditional Vedic interpretations for informational purposes only.
⚖️ Not Professional Advice
Predictions, dosha analysis, remedies, and Muhurta suggestions are based on traditional astrology principles and should not be treated as medical, legal, financial, or professional advice.
🏥 Medical Decisions
Delivery timing and childbirth-related guidance must always be discussed with qualified medical professionals.
🎯 No Guarantee of Accuracy
The app does not guarantee prediction accuracy or specific outcomes. Users are responsible for their own decisions and interpretations.
⚠️ Limitation of Liability
By using this app, you agree that Nakshatra Speaks and its creators are not liable for any direct or indirect consequences arising from the use of the app.
